correct_answer: Communities used local solar time before standardized time measurement.
Trains and telegraphs highlighted the inefficiencies of using local time across long distances.
The introduction of standard time in the mid-19th century marked a major development in timekeeping.
Railway Time was adopted by British railway companies in 1847.
Sir Sandford Fleming proposed dividing the world into 24 time zones in the late 19th century.
The International Meridian Conference in 1884 agreed to adopt a system of standard time zones based on Greenwich Mean Time.
The Greenwich Meridian in London was chosen as the Prime Meridian for coordinating time globally.
